title: Rapper Flavor Flav donates sports equipment to Cambridge youth center
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Flavor Flav donated sports equipment to Frisoli Youth Center in Cambridge, supporting community after-school and evening programs.
The donation included basketballs, dodgeballs, volleyball nets, and more, benefiting youths aged 9 to 19.
Flavor Flav collaborated with Harvard College and Franklin Sports for the donation to the city-run center.

title: Jaylen Brown has a candid answer when asked if he deserves NBA MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown averages 28.8 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists for the Celtics this season.
Brown helped the Celtics achieve a 54-26 record, securing the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference.
Despite not being an MVP favorite, Brown received support from notable figures like Paul Pierce and Tony Kornheiser.

title: Special World Cup ticket sales shatter Commuter Rail records in first day
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Over 17,450 special Commuter Rail tickets sold on the first day for World Cup matches at Boston Stadium.
The MBTA will operate 14 trains per match, accommodating up to 20,000 riders across seven World Cup matches.
The $80 tickets allow unlimited travel on all Commuter Rail lines on World Cup match days for additional flexibility.

title: The Boston Fleet are ready to take a sold-out TD Garden by storm
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Fleet will play their first game at TD Garden on April 12 against the Montreal Victoire.
Saturday's game is sold out, marking one of the highest-attended games in PWHL history, with over 17,800 expected.
Olympic gold medalists Megan Keller, Aerin Frankel, and Haley Winn will be featured at the sold-out event.

title: Fall River woman who led sex trafficking ring with 5 others pleads guilty
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Christy Parker, 28, of Fall River, pleaded guilty to leading a sex trafficking ring victimizing minors and an adult in 2023.
Parker and co-defendants trafficked victims in Massachusetts, Rhode Island, and South Carolina, using coercion and promises of 'easy money'.
Parker faces up to life in prison and fines for charges including sex trafficking by force and conspiracy to commit sex trafficking.

title: Police look to identify owners of 2 dogs found dead on South Shore
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two dead dogs found in Weymouth, one was a small white Terrier-type without a collar or microchip.
Police consider the death of a medium-sized, black and white Pitbull mix found in a plastic bag suspicious.
Authorities ask for public help to identify owners, contact Animal Control at 781-660-3707 with information.

title: She’s running Boston in memory of her son, and for  necrotizing enterocolitis
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jennifer Canvasser is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of her son and for the NEC Society.
Canvasser founded the NEC Society after her son Micah died from necrotizing enterocolitis before his first birthday.
Following the marathon, Canvasser will present on NEC care at the Pediatric Academic Societies Meeting in Boston.

title: Braintree man sentenced to life in prison for murdering Salem State student 40 years ago
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Carey, 66, sentenced to life for the 1986 murder of Salem State student Claire Gravel with no parole.
Carey indicted in 2022 and found guilty of strangling Gravel in 1986, identified through DNA evidence.
Gravel's body was found on Route 128 in Beverly; the case went unsolved until new evidence emerged in 2012.

title: ‘It’s pure excitement’: What James Hagens said after first practice with Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, a 19-year-old prospect, has joined the Boston Bruins and could play on Saturday.
Hagens signed an entry-level contract and impressed with 23 goals in 34 games as a Boston College sophomore.
Coach Marco Sturm is cautious about Hagens's NHL debut, emphasizing readiness and team needs before playing him.

title: Report: 76ers star Joel Embiid to undergo surgery for appendicitis
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Philadelphia 76ers star Joel Embiid will have surgery for appendicitis, potentially affecting his availability for the playoffs.
The 76ers, currently seventh in the Eastern Conference, are fighting to avoid the play-in tournament.
Embiid has averaged 26.9 points, 7.7 rebounds, and 3.9 assists in 38 games this season.
